stories.headline: SEC-CFTC name BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, LINK as digital commodities in joint interpretation
stories.tldr: A joint SEC-CFTC interpretation lays out a unified token taxonomy and explicitly designates the five biggest assets as digital commodities.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: This kills the Howey ambiguity that has shadowed major altcoins for a decade and clears the runway for staked-asset ETFs and bank custody.
the_details: Joint SEC-CFTC interpretation defines five token classes: digital commodities, collectibles, tools, stablecoins, and digital securities.
BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP and LINK are named directly as digital commodities, putting them firmly under CFTC oversight.
Agencies signed a Memorandum of Understanding to coordinate enforcement and rulemaking across the new framework.

stories.headline: BTC slides to $75,752 as Trump weighs new Iran strikes; ETH cracks $2,252
stories.tldr: Bitcoin opened at its lowest level in over a week and ethereum at a two-week low after reports of fresh US military planning around Iran.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Crypto is trading like a high-beta risk asset again; geopolitical headlines now move BTC harder than the Fed does.
the_details: BTC opened at $75,752.12 Thursday — lowest open in over a week — and recovered to $76,130 by 7:18 a.m. ET.
ETH opened at $2,252.45, the weakest print since April 13, before clawing back to $2,268.50.
Trigger: reports Trump is weighing additional Iran action to force renewed peace talks and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Pump.fun torches $370M in PUMP, commits 50% of revenue to perpetual buyback-burn
stories.tldr: Solana memecoin launchpad Pump.fun burned $370M of PUMP and locked in a permanent buyback-burn funded by half of platform revenue.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: This is the most aggressive token-revenue alignment yet from a top-five fee generator and sets a new bar for memecoin platform tokenomics.
the_details: $370M of PUMP tokens already burned; future burns will run as a buyback-and-burn program.
50% of net revenue earmarked for buybacks; remaining 50% reserved for 'big bets' over the next 5-10 years.
Pump.fun has been a top fee generator on Solana, pivoting from pure mercenary supply to value capture.

stories.headline: Banks lobby Treasury to slow GENIUS Act rollout; seek 60-day comment extension
stories.tldr: Banking groups asked Treasury and FDIC to extend comment periods on three GENIUS Act stablecoin rules until after the OCC finishes its own framework.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A successful delay drags issuer licensing into late 2026, bullish for incumbent USDC and USDT, bearish for bank-issued stablecoin contenders.
the_details: Treasury, FinCEN and OFAC issued GENIUS Act proposals on April 1, 7, and 8 covering reserves, AML, and state-vs-federal jurisdiction.
Dual track: issuers under $10B can opt into qualifying state regimes; above $10B must move to OCC supervision.
Bankers want 60-day extensions stacked on top of the OCC's parallel rulemaking before any final framework lands.

stories.headline: Solana ETFs draw $11.3M weekly inflow as Israel approves SOL-native stablecoin BILS
stories.tldr: SOL ETFs recorded $11.3M of weekly inflows while Israel greenlit BILS, the first regulated stablecoin issued on Solana.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: ETF demand is fading even with regulatory wins — SOL needs a real consumer payments moment, not just policy tailwinds, to retake $100.
the_details: Solana ETFs pulled $11.31M last week but April total of $39.93M is the sixth straight monthly decline.
Israel approved BILS stablecoin on Solana on April 28, the first sovereign-tier stablecoin issued natively on the chain.
SOL trading near $85.97 on Binance with the Alpenglow ~150ms finality upgrade still ahead as a 2026 catalyst.
